title: Arachnaria macrura
taxonomy: Arachnaria macrura is a type of deep-sea coral, and as such, it belongs to the phylum Cnidaria. This phylum is divided into several classes, including Anthozoa, which includes corals and sea anemones. Arachnaria macrura is classified as a member of the family Arachnariidae, which is part of the order Pennatulacea. The Pennatulacea order is a group of deep-sea corals characterized by their long, slender stems and feathery or branching colonies. Further classification would place Arachnaria macrura within the kingdom Animalia.
uses: Arachnaria macrura is actually not a recognized plant.
